Alzheimer's disease and neuroinflammation: a comprehensive review

Neurosci Lett. 2024 Jan 15;789:123-130. PMID: 12345678 PMCID: PMC9876543 PMCID: PMC9876543
  • Department of Neuroscience, University of California, San Francisco, CA, USA.
  • Institute for Neurodegenerative Diseases, Stanford University, Stanford, CA, USA.
  • Department of Neurology, Johns Hopkins University, Baltimore, MD, USA.
BACKGROUND:

Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disorder characterized by progressive cognitive decline. Neuroinflammation has emerged as a key contributor to disease pathogenesis.

METHODS:

We conducted a systematic review of literature published between 2010 and 2023, analyzing studies on inflammatory markers and therapeutic interventions in AD.

RESULTS:

Our analysis revealed significant upregulation of pro-inflammatory cytokines in AD patients. Anti-inflammatory treatments showed promising results in early clinical trials.

CONCLUSION:

Neuroinflammation plays a crucial role in AD progression. Targeting inflammatory pathways may offer novel therapeutic strategies for AD treatment.

Keywords: Alzheimer's disease; neuroinflammation; cytokines; microglia; therapeutic targets; neurodegeneration.
